D1 Optics L3 Apply Reference

Young's Double-Slit Interference

set slit spacing and wavelength, then read the bright-fringe spacing on the screen

Two coherent slits act as twin wave sources. Where their path difference is a whole number of wavelengths, light adds; where it is a half, it cancels.

Adjust slit separation d, wavelength λ, and screen distance L. The wavefronts on the left interfere; the right strip shows the fringe pattern and the gold readout gives the measured spacing Δy.
